MeshBot Node (Meshtastic Interface) 

#generated by why0712 using ChatGpt tool.

MeshBot is a lightweight Python bot designed for Meshtastic LoRa mesh networks. It runs on a device connected via serial (typically /dev/ttyACM0) and listens for incoming text messages, processes commands, and responds over the mesh.

The bot includes basic system information commands, signal diagnostics (RSSI and SNR), and a simple interactive simulation game mode. It is designed to run on low-power devices such as Raspberry Pi.

FEATURES

Meshtastic serial interface support
Command-based interaction system
Signal reporting (RSSI / SNR)
Simple turn-based simulation game mode
Full terminal logging (RX / TX / CMD / GAME / ERROR)
Lightweight and easy to modify

REQUIREMENTS

Python 3

Dependencies:

meshtastic
pubsub

Install with:
pip install meshtastic pubsub

The device must be accessible as a serial interface, usually:
/dev/ttyACM0

If permission errors occur:

ensure user is in dialout group:
sudo usermod -aG dialout $USER
then reboot

Optional system fix if port is locked:
sudo systemctl stop ModemManager

RUNNING THE BOT

Run the script with:
python3 pingbot.py

COMMANDS

General commands:

help
Shows available commands

info
Displays basic bot information

time
Shows current system time

uptime
Shows system uptime

nodes
Shows number of known mesh nodes

ping
Returns connection quality:

RSSI (signal strength)
SNR (signal noise ratio)

game
Starts simulation mode

SIMULATION MODE

Start:
game

Menu options:
1 - Start simulation
2 - Load scenario
3 - Random event

During simulation:
A - Stabilize system
B - Analyze signals
C - Execute protocol
quit - Exit simulation

The simulation runs for a limited number of turns and generates random system events with changing stability scores.

LOGGING

The bot prints all activity to the terminal:

RX = received messages
TX = outgoing messages
CMD = parsed commands
GAME = simulation events
ERROR = system errors

Example:
[12:00:01] [RX] node123: ping
[12:00:01] [CMD] node123: ping
[12:00:01] [TX] PONG

FILE LOGGING (OPTIONAL)

Logs can be saved to a file (meshbot.log) by enabling file logging in the script.

To view logs live:
tail -f meshbot.log

NOTES

Only one process can use /dev/ttyACM0 at a time.
If the port is busy, another Meshtastic process is likely running.

FUTURE IDEAS

Web dashboard for live node monitoring
Multi-node simulation gameplay
AI-based auto responses
Packet history analysis
Docker deployment support
